success_surface: Duplicate row removal based on single-column criteria with proper tool selection and error recovery
effective_behaviors: Initial data exploration before manipulation to understand structure, detect duplicates, and verify scope
Simplification of complex operations when errors occur (removing unnecessary style preservation when causing RecursionError)
Using appropriate iteration strategy with reverse sorting for row deletions to prevent index shifting
Implementing backup and safe write procedures with try-except error handling
Clear verification output showing original counts, changes made, and final state for validation
Correct column indexing awareness (1-indexed vs 0-indexed) when accessing cells across different libraries
Processing data rows excluding headers when performing transformations
Tracking duplicate detection using set-based value comparison rather than full-row comparison
evidence: Agent first loaded full dataframe with pandas.read_excel() to examine shape, columns, and identify duplicates before implementing solution
When RecursionError occurred during style copying, agent removed unnecessary complexity and refocused on core deduplication logic
Rows to delete were sorted in descending order (reverse=True) before deletion to avoid index offset problems
Created backup_path and used shutil.copy() + try-except block around wb.save() operation
Printed comprehensive summary including Original total rows, Unique Teacher IDs, Duplicate rows detected, Rows deleted, Remaining rows
Used ws.cell(row=row_idx, column=1).value correctly accessing Column A with 1-indexed column parameter
Started iteration from row 2 (min_row=2 or range(2, max_row+1)) to skip header row
Used seen_values set to track Column A values only rather than comparing entire row content

memory_items.title: Pre-Manipulation Data Exploration
memory_items.lesson: Always inspect workbook structure before transformation
memory_items.generalization: Load and examine spreadsheet schema, row/column counts, data types, and potential edge cases (duplicates, empty cells) before applying modifications to inform strategy and validate results.

memory_items.title: Simplified Operation After Error
memory_items.lesson: Strip non-essential operations when encountering complex library errors
memory_items.generalization: If deepcopy or complex object manipulation causes recursion/memory errors, remove those steps and implement simpler alternatives that preserve core functionality while maintaining basic data integrity.

memory_items.title: Reverse-Sort Deletion Order
memory_items.lesson: Delete rows in descending order to maintain index integrity
memory_items.generalization: When removing multiple rows programmatically, sort target indices in reverse (descending) order to prevent index shifts that would cause subsequent deletions to affect wrong rows.

memory_items.title: Safe Write with Backup
memory_items.lesson: Create backup copy before modifying and save with error handling
memory_items.generalization: Always copy original file to temporary or backup location before modifications; wrap save operations in try-except blocks with fallback restoration to prevent data loss from runtime errors.

memory_items.title: Comprehensive Validation Output
memory_items.generalization: After executing modifications, print summary statistics including original vs final row/column counts, number of affected records, specific changes made, and sample verification output to confirm correctness.

edits.content:
## Trace-Derived Lessons

### Data Exploration First
- Always load and examine complete spreadsheet structure (shape, headers, data types, existing duplicates) before implementing transformations to inform strategy and enable post-validation.

### Error-Driven Simplification
- If complex object operations (deepcopy of styles, formulas, merged cell structures) cause RecursionError or memory issues, strip non-essential operations and retain only core data modification functionality.

### Reverse-Order Row Deletion
- When removing multiple rows via code, always sort target row indices in descending order before deletion to prevent subsequent deletions from affecting incorrect rows due to index shifting.

### Safe Write Protocol
- Create a backup copy of the original file before any modifications; use try-except blocks around save operations with automatic restoration from backup if write fails to ensure no data corruption.

### Verification Reporting
- Print transformation summary after execution including: original count, modified count, specific cells/rows affected, and sample verification output to enable independent validation of correctness.
